title: Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva is situated on the banks of the Periyar river at Kalady of Ernakulam District in Kerala state in India.
overview.description: Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva is situated on the banks of the Periyar river at Kalady of Ernakulam District in Kerala state in India. The Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple is one of the important temples in Cochin Kingdom. According to folklore, sage Parashurama has installed the idol of Shiva in the Treta Yuga. The temple is a part of the 108 Shiva Temples in Kerala.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Cheranallur
topPlaces.description: Cheranallur is a suburb of Kochi city in the state of Kerala, India and lies on the banks of the Periyar River. According to tradition, the area was named by its earlier inhabitants who found this place very fertile and beautiful.

topPlaces.name: Kottamam
topPlaces.description: Kottamam is a city situated in the banks of the holy river Periyar in Ernakulam district, Kerala state, south India. It is situated about 9.5 km from Angamaly and 3 km from Kalady, the birthplace of Adi Sankaracharya.

topPlaces.name: Thottuva Dhanwanthari Temple
topPlaces.description: Thottuva Dhanwanthari temple is a Hindu temple located in Thottuva, Kerala, India. Thottuva temple is situated on the Perumbavoor- Kodanad route, at about one kilometre away from Thottuva junction in Koovapady panchayat at Ernakulam district.

topPlaces.name: Naduvattam (Ernakulam)
topPlaces.description: Naduvattam is a small village situated in Ernakulam district, Kerala state, south India. It is situated about 12 km from Angamaly and 5 km from Kalady.

topPlaces.name: Cheranalloor East
topPlaces.description: Cheranalloor (East) is a village in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India. It lies on the banks of Periyar River near Kalady.

topPlaces.name: Thottuva
topPlaces.description: Thottuva is a village in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India. It lies on the banks of Periyar River near Kalady.

topPlaces.name: Karppillikkavu Sree Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Karppillikkavu Sree Mahadeva Temple is a temple in India, dedicated to Shiva as kirata (hunter). This temple's specialty is that the deity give dharshan to the western side.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (8 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (31 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cheranalloor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Neeleeswaram, Cheranallur, Kottamam, Thottuva Dhanwanthari Temple, Naduvattam (Ernakulam).
